title: clearSyntaxProperties()
text: clearSyntaxProperties() Restores the initial values of a field's syntax properties. FieldObject . clearSyntaxProperties() Arguments None Exceptions The following exceptions may be thrown: uniface.InvalidDataObjectException Description The initial values of a DSP field's widget properties can be set in the Define (Component) Field Properties dialog, or they can be set in Proc using $fieldsyntax . The can be retrieved in JavaScript using the getDeclarativeSyntax function. Once the DSP is loaded, the values of these properties can be dynamically changed in the browser using the setSyntaxProperty and setSyntaxProperties API functions. You can use the clearSyntaxProperties function to reset the field's syntax properties to their initial values. Related Links Field Syntax Codes Field syntax codes specify the data entry pattern, the allowed characters, and whether fields are mandatory or read-only. clearSyntaxProperty() Restores the initial value of the specified syntax property. getSyntaxProperties() Retrieves field syntax properties that were set in the browser (using the setSyntaxProperty or setSyntaxProperties functions). setSyntaxProperties() Sets multiple syntax properties for a field. uniface.Field The uniface.Field object provides access to selected properties of the field that it represents. It is returned by the getField function of uniface.Occurrence. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
